result:
figcheck passes and the pre-registration is complete. Summary of this iteration's design:

**Experiment 20 (pre-registered in `runs/pending_experiment.json`): per-epoch training-set resampling** — category `training`.

**Why this experiment:** The research history shows the best metric at 1054.87 m (exp 17) with a flat per-lighting-bucket error profile, so lighting is no longer the leading term. The exp-18 probe pinned the bottleneck: a ~3× held-out-location gap (train-split crops localize to 267–400 m median, eval-split to 708–1153 m). The last two experiments attacked that gap and were both reverted — exp 18 architecturally (per-patch votes destroyed the global arrangement signal) and exp 19 via content corruption (distractor patches made training strictly harder). Jointly they say: keep the whole-crop signal intact and the content clean. So I attacked the same bottleneck along the one untouched axis: **repetition**.

**The change:** the kept trainer builds one frozen tensor — 6,000 locations per bucket, each at one heading and one relight realization — and passes over it 8 times, which makes per-crop template memorization the cheapest fit for the 573k-param 1024-way head; memorized templates are exactly what fails to transfer to eval blocks, and ~39k enumerable train locations are never seen at all. The redesign redraws the 6,000 locations, headings, and realization assignments fresh at the start of every epoch (rng seeded `[seed, epoch]`; the two extra relight realizations are rendered once and cached as PNGs). Same per-epoch cost and step count, but ~30k distinct locations seen per bucket (~68% of 45k), each ~1–2× and never twice identically — memorization stops paying, and only location-transferable cues can reduce the loss. This is the training-scale family (exp 7, kept), materially distinct from refuted exp 6, which resampled only rotations of the same 800 locations pre-trunk/pre-C4.

**Prediction:** worst-case median 1054.87 → ~850–950 m if the memorization diagnosis is right; failure signature is persistent high training loss with the metric regressing to ≥1100 m.

Everything else is untouched: `model/model.py` byte-identical (so the 3.3 MB ONNX and 2.7 ms latency gates are unaffected), optimizer, loss, calibration, export, and CLI contracts all restated in the implementation brief. The architecture figure reuses the kept lineage's inference path verbatim with the change drawn red in the training lane, and passes figcheck. The harness takes it from here — implement, train, score, keep or revert.

tool_input.command: grep -n "EPOCHS\|AREAS=" autoresearch/loop.sh | head; echo ---; ls runs/20260721_175707_iter1/; cat runs/20260721_175707_iter1/train_info.json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(json.dumps(d,indent=1)[:1500])"
tool_input.description: Check epoch count and last run's training stats
Check training wall time, GPU, and RAM

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=19;" > /tmp/exp19.svg; wc -c /tmp/exp19.svg
tool_input.description: Export exp 19 architecture SVG for reference
